---
format: revealjs
---

## Today's Agenda {background-image="libs/Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

```{r}
# background-size="1920px 1080px"
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(kableExtra)
```

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}

**Final Paper Preperations**

- Applying and evaluating the models

:::

<br>

::: r-stack
Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
:::

::: notes
Prep for Class

1. Review submitted work on Canvas (notes from applying models to the cases)

:::




## Final Exam (Due May 6, 10am) {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center}

<br>

Select a **NEW** international political event and make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we've studied in class this term "best" explains the event. 

Your paper must consider and evaluate how **THREE** of the IR theories from class explain your chosen event.

- No double-dipping on realism/liberalism

- At least one theory from the second half of class

- No events used in the prior paper

::: notes

**Questions on the assignment?**

:::



## For Today {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center}

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}

1. Submit a description of your chosen event, and

2. Use each theory to explain your event.

:::

::: notes

On Monday we made sure everyone had a clear event and had selected three models

<br>

Today we make sure each of the three components of the final paper are in really good shape.

<br>

By the end of class today you should have:

- An international political event to explain supported by high quality evidence

- A simple explanation of the event using three models of IR, and

- A start on the strengths and weaknesses of each model applied to your event

<br>

**Do our goals for today make sense?**

<br>

*Split class into groups of 3*

- Go sit with your group
:::



## {background-image="libs/Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
**Focus on one paper at a time (15 mins)**
:::

1. Is this a clear international political event?

2. Is each model being used appropriately?

3. Brainstorm strengths and weaknesses of each model applied to this event

::: notes

Focus on each paper for 15 minutes (use all the time!)

- Review their event and their use of the three models to explain it

- Help them brainstorm strengths and weaknesses for each model applied to this event

<br>

**Questions on the assignment?**

- I'll tell you when to move to the next paper

- Go!

:::




## For Friday: Outline {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center .smaller}

**Intro**

+ What is the question?
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis statement

**Apply Theory 1**

+ Evidence

**Apply Theory 2**

+ Evidence

**Apply Theory 3**

+ Evidence

::: notes

Outline due in class on Friday

- Submit as an assignment, NOT a Canvas discussion posting

- Bring your outline to class

<br>

### Questions?

- Get to it!
:::
